MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-17-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth N. Branham of HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG (415-766-3200) appearing for defendant and moving party John Crane Inc.
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties Lisa Castillo as Successor-In-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Abraham Castillo, Deceased; Abraham Castillo Jr, Oscar Castillo as wrongful death heirs of Abraham Castillo, Deceased.

RULING: DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Crane Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment or, In the Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. The Court did not consider Defendant's reply papers for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7(B).) As to Plaintiff's design defect claims, Defendant failed to demonstrate that it supplied a specific product to the Navy pursuant to a contract and specific MIL-SPEC that required asbestos between January 1980 through October 14, 1982, as not all packing MIL-SEPCS specified asbestos as a required ingredient. (Springs Declaration, Ex. H [MIL-P-17303C].) As to Plaintiff's failure to warn claims, Defendant failed to demonstrate that it complied with the Navy's warning requirements between 1980 and 1982. Captain Margaret McCloskey's declaration is negated by evidence from Plaintiff demonstrating that MIL-STD-129G was superseded by MIL-STD-129H on January 3, 1978, amended on July 1, 1980. (Rose Declaration, Ex. 12.) Defendant's alternative failure to warn theory that no warning would have reached Plaintiff or changed his use of packing products is speculative. As to Plaintiff's claims of exposure at Butcher Boys, Defendants failed to demonstrate that it conducted comprehensive discovery to determine Plaintiff's evidence on this issue. (See Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1433, 1440.) Court overruled objection to plaintiffs' evidence on exhibit 12. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Raquel G. Sharp, CSR # 10619, Raquel@transcriptlegal.com (Tel: 925-384-0003) (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT John Crane Incs Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication On Plaintiffs Claim For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Oct-30-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Bill D. Fountain of HAWKINS PARNELL THACKSTON & YOUNG (415-766-3202) appearing for defendant and moving party John Crane Inc.
Gilbert L. Purcell of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing party Oscar Castillo, Abraham Castillo Jr. as wrongful death heirs, and Lisa Castillo, As Successor-In-Interest To And As Wrongful Death Heir Of Abraham Castillo, Deceased.


RULING: DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Crane Inc.'s Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied. Defendant John Crane Inc. failed to shift burden. A defendant may move for summary judgment by showing "through factually devoid discovery response that the plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence." (Collins v. CalPortland Co. (2014) 228 Cal.App.4th 582, 588-89 [citing Aguilar v. Atlantic Co. (2001) 24 Cal.4th 826 854-855; citing Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 96, 101].) If a plaintiff responds "to comprehensive interrogatories seeking all known facts with boilerplate answers that restate their allegations, or simply provide laundry lists of people and/or documents, the burden of production will almost certainly be shifted to them once defendants move for summary judgment and properly present plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses." (Andrews v. Foster Wheeler, supra, 138 Cal.App.4th at 107.) A claim for punitive damages under Civil Code section 3294 requires a showing that John Crane "carried on despicable conduct with an awareness of the probable dangerous consequences, and willfully failed to avoid such consequences." (Pfeifer v. John Crane (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1299-1301[quoting Angie M. v. Superior Court (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 1217, 1228].) Here, Plaintiffs' discovery responses provide particularized facts and evidence that support a claim for punitive damages. (See Ex. Q, Ex. R, Ex. S, Ex. T, Ex. U, and Ex. V.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Kelly Roemer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 6435, kroemer@aikenwelch.com (Tel: 877-451-1580) (503/GLW)
